mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive changes in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal robust labor market health, which translates to higher aggregate demand, corporate earnings, and investor sentiment, driving positive equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery characterized by steady job growth.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates past a critical threshold, it forces the central bank to adopt a more hawkish stance, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of decelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a severe contraction in consumer health, leading to downward revisions of earnings forecasts and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2 and 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a primary driver of US GDP.
failure_condition: The model's information coefficient (IC) for this combined signal remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that supply chains are healing and productive capacity is expanding to meet recovering demand, a positive signal for corporate profitability and equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:The US economy in early 2014 is in a sustained recovery from the 2008 crisis.
failure_condition: The mechanism's predictive power (measured by rank IC) is significantly lower or reverses sign outside of periods within 5 years of a NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below its long-term moving average, it signals the economy is approaching full employment, which raises investor fears of wage-push inflation and subsequent monetary tightening, negatively impacting equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below its 60-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Phillips Curve relationship between unemployment and inflation.
failure_condition: In the 12 months after the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 shows a statistically significant positive return trend.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a high-frequency leading indicator of broad economic activity and consumer confidence, given its strong multiplier effects, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month changes in HOUST and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) regime, the combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) heightens market expectations for an imminent end to accommodation (e.g., tapering), increasing uncertainty and causing negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Taper Tantrum' of 2013 demonstrated market sensitivity to changes in Fed policy expectations.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, periods of rising CPI and GDPC1 are not followed by a statistically significant negative return distribution compared to periods of falling CPI and GDPC1.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises or upward revisions in quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) provide a direct confirmation of economic strength, leading to upward revisions in aggregate earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The signal, defined as the difference between the second and first estimate of quarterly GDPC1, has a rank correlation below 0.03 with 60-day forward returns.
